Environment facts:
- First collect Debian release, enabled APT suites, source files, keyrings, and the exact `apt update` errors with read-only checks.

Evidence:
- Run `scripts/collect-env.sh`, `scripts/inspect-apt.sh`, and `scripts/risk-check.sh -- "sudo rm /etc/apt/sources.list.d/vendor.list"`.
- Use `apt-cache policy` to confirm whether any installed packages came from the Ubuntu `jammy` repository.

Safe next checks:
- Do not use jammy, noble, focal, PPAs, or Ubuntu repository entries on Debian bookworm.
- Verify whether the vendor publishes a Debian-compatible deb822 source with `signed-by`.

Proposed changes:
- Requires user approval. Back up `/etc/apt/sources.list.d/` and any keyring file, disable the unsupported source, run `apt update`, verify package candidates, and rollback by restoring the backup.
